description: Banking trojan that disguises itself as PDF readers, QR scanners, or file managers. Uses accessibility services to perform on-device fraud and overlay attacks on banking apps.
source_reference: Public threat intel reports on Anatsa/Teabot family (2023-2024)

description: Banking trojan targeting credentials via fake login overlays, can intercept SMS/OTP and capture keystrokes. Often distributed via fake Chrome/Play Store updates.
source_reference: Public threat intel reports on SOVA banking trojan (2022-2023)

description: SMS-spreading banking trojan that requests permission to be the default SMS app, harvests contacts to spread further, and shows fake banking login overlays.
source_reference: Public threat intel reports on FluBot (2021-2022)

description: Disguises as utility apps (QR code reader, PDF viewer), uses accessibility services for screen recording/overlay to steal banking credentials and 2FA codes.
source_reference: Public threat intel reports on TeaBot/Anatsa overlap (2021-2023)

description: Spyware/banking trojan capable of factory-resetting devices after fraud (to destroy evidence), uses accessibility services for full screen monitoring and GPS tracking.
source_reference: Public threat intel reports on Brata (2022)
